rules.text: Return a complete work order covering: Feature Name & Goal, User Story Data Points, Requirement Traceability, File Structure, State Management, Form Model & Validation (when applicable), UI/UX Requirements, Acceptance Criteria.
Use concrete data points from user story (routes, fields, selectors, constraints). Mark non-story details under Assumptions.
UI/UX Requirements must include a Template Pattern References line. If none fit, use 'Template Pattern References: none' and add Deviation Notes.
If user story lists required app-* selectors, include a checklist mapping each selector to a feature location or interaction.
For form-centric stories, include typed Reactive Form schema, field-by-field validation matrix, error timing, submit rules, and non-CVA value/valueChange binding strategy.
Explicitly instruct Developer to overwrite app shell routes and ensure the new feature is first page shown.
Call out reusable defects as Template/Agent-level when issue likely belongs to template, shared UI wrappers, or orchestration defaults.
Continue until npm run build succeeds with zero errors and zero Angular warnings.
Overwrite src/app/app.html and src/app/app.routes.ts so feature route loads first. Update app.ts and app.spec.ts to remove default boilerplate assumptions.
Use OnPush, inject(), input/output/model APIs, native @if/@for control flow, and Signals for feature state.
Never use ngModel/template-driven forms. Use typed Reactive Forms with explicit validators and visible validation UX.
For shared controls without CVA support, bind [value] and (valueChange) explicitly; do not use formControlName unless control supports CVA.
Use prebuilt components from src/app/shared/ui (prefer barrel import src/app/shared/ui). Avoid duplicating existing shared primitives.
If user story/work order requires selectors, ensure each required selector appears meaningfully in feature templates.
Use Tailwind utilities in templates. Keep feature CSS minimal; do not redefine utility classes like container/grid/flex.
On retry, prioritize minimal targeted edits for listed failures before any broader refactor.
If failures indicate reusable template/shared UI defects, classify and report as Template/Agent-level issue with path and root cause.
Run npm run test -- --watch=false and inspect code semantically even after deterministic checks pass.
Validate shared UI reuse by actual selectors/imports, not guessed app-* file paths.
For form-centric stories, verify typed Reactive Form model, validators, error UI states, submit handling, and non-CVA bridging.
If required selectors are listed, verify each selector exists in feature templates and is used meaningfully.
Fail only explicit required criteria from user story/work order. Do not fail optional or speculative requirements.
Classify every failure as Feature-level or Template/Agent-level with concrete file paths and next fix steps.
Output exactly PASS only when all checks pass with no caveats.
